name: Vía Verde de Bazagona — circular por la Dehesa del Carrascal
region_intro: Jogging around Malpartida De Plasencia offers access to diverse natural landscapes, including the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve and expansive dehesa ecosystems. The region features varied terrain, from the flat paths of the dehesas to more undulating routes with elevation changes. Natural springs and historical sites like Cerro de los Castillejos add points of interest along many running paths. Best jogging routes around Malpartida De Plasencia The most popular jogging route is Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia – circular by the Jerte river, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the scenic Jerte river. Another top favourite among local runners is Paseo del Pantano — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This route offers views around the Pantano de Plasencia reservoir. Local runners also love the Cerro Gimio – circular via the Arroyo Malvecino Route, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 2 minutes. Jogging around Malpartida De Plasencia is defined by Mediterranean forests, dehesa landscapes, and riverine paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside runs to more challenging trails with elevation. The routes in Malpartida De Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Malpartida De Plasencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Malpartida de Plasencia?
A: There are over 80 dedicated running routes around Malpartida de Plasencia, offering a wide variety of distances and terrains for all preferences.

Q: Are there running trails suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy jog?
A: Yes, the region offers options for all ability levels. You can find several easy routes, including those along riverine paths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990933871/paseo-fluvial-de-plasencia-circular-by-the-jerte-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo Fluvial de Plasencia – circular by the Jerte river&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.2 km trail that follows the scenic Jerte river, offering a relatively flat and enjoyable experience.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the jogging routes?
A: Jogging around Malpartida de Plasencia offers diverse natural landscapes. You'll encounter Mediterranean forests, expansive dehesa ecosystems with holm oaks, and riverine paths. Many routes also provide views of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve and National Park, known for its exceptional birdlife.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295142952/paseo-del-pantano-circular-by-the-pantano-de-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo del Pantano — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 12.1 km path that offers scenic views around the Plasencia reservoir.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails with elevation gain?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more challenging run, there are routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990923341/cerro-gimio-circular-via-the-arroyo-malvecino-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Gimio – circular via the Arroyo Malvecino Route&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 7.8 km trail leading through varied terrain with notable ascents.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: The region is rich in points of interest. You might encounter natural springs, ancient trees, and historical sites like the remains of a Vetton settlement and menhirs around Cerro de los Castillejos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6090688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible along some routes.

Q: Is Malpartida de Plasencia a good place for long-distance running?
A: Yes, the region offers several longer routes. The historic Vía de la Plata, an ancient Roman road, passes nearby and provides long, relatively flat stretches ideal for extended jogs. Additionally, routes within the Monfragüe National Park can be combined for longer distances.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Malpartida de Plasencia?
A: The running routes in Malpartida de Plasencia are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from peaceful riverside paths to challenging trails through Mediterranean forests, and the opportunity to experience the natural beauty of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features?
A: Yes, many routes incorporate water features. Besides the Jerte river, you can find trails around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6090688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1295142952/paseo-del-pantano-circular-by-the-pantano-de-plasencia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paseo del Pantano — circular by the Pantano de Plasencia&lt;/a&gt;. The municipal area also features numerous natural springs, particularly in the Dehesa Boyal 'El Robledo', which can be found along various paths.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Malpartida de Plasencia?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn particularly pleasant for running, with milder temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Spring is especially beautiful when the Jerte Valley, nearby, is famous for its cherry blossoms. Even in summer, early mornings or late evenings offer cooler conditions for a run.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly scenic or offer great viewpoints?
A: Many routes offer scenic views, especially those that traverse higher ground or skirt the Monfragüe National Park. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Higuerilla Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and other spots overlooking the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6090688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plasencia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; provide excellent panoramic vistas during your run.

Q: Can I find running routes that are less crowded?
A: While popular routes like those along the Jerte river can see more activity, the extensive network of trails, especially within the dehesas and less-traveled paths of the Monfragüe Biosphere Reserve, offers plenty of opportunities for a peaceful and solitary run.
tips: This was an old railway line that has now been made into a green, beautiful and quiet road. Surrounded by dehesa forests typical of this area of Extremadura.
